children.description:
The file format version.
Represented as a string including the semver major, minor version numbers (and optionally the meta tag). For example: "0.4", "1.0-rc.2", "1.0" (after stable release).
See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-configuration/blob/main/VERSIONING.md for more details.
The yaml format is documented at https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-configuration/tree/main/schema
children.label: File Format

children.description: Configure if the SDK is disabled or not.
children.label: Disabled

children.description: Configure the log level of the internal logger used by the SDK.
children.label: Log Level

children.description:
Configure max attribute value size. 
Value must be non-negative.
children.label: Attribute Value Length Limit

children.description:
Configure max attribute count. 
Value must be non-negative.
children.label: Attribute Count Limit

children.defaultBehavior: default values as described in AttributeLimits are used
children.description: Configure general attribute limits. See also tracer_provider.limits, logger_provider.limits.
children.label: Attribute Limits

children.description: Configure log record processors.

Configure delay interval (in milliseconds) between two consecutive exports. 
Value must be non-negative.
children.label: Schedule Delay

children.description:
Configure maximum allowed time (in milliseconds) to export data. 
Value must be non-negative. A value of 0 indicates no limit (infinity).
children.label: Export Timeout

children.description: Configure maximum queue size. Value must be positive.
children.label: Max Queue Size

children.description: Configure maximum batch size. Value must be positive.
children.label: Max Export Batch Size

children.description: Configure exporter.
children.label: Exporter

children.defaultBehavior: the http://localhost:4318/v1/{signal} (where signal is 'traces', 'logs', or 'metrics') is used
children.description: Configure endpoint, including the signal specific path.
children.label: Endpoint

children.defaultBehavior: system default certificate verification is used for secure connections
children.description:
Configure certificate used to verify a server's TLS credentials. 
Absolute path to certificate file in PEM format.
children.label: Ca File

children.description:
Configure mTLS private client key. 
Absolute path to client key file in PEM format. If set, .client_certificate must also be set.
children.label: Key File

children.description:
Configure mTLS client certificate. 
Absolute path to client certificate file in PEM format. If set, .client_key must also be set.
children.label: Cert File

children.defaultBehavior: system default TLS settings are used
children.description: Configure TLS settings for the exporter.
children.label: Tls

children.description:
Configure headers. Entries have higher priority than entries from .headers_list.
If an entry's .value is null, the entry is ignored.
The name of the pair.
children.label: Name

children.description: The value of the pair.
children.label: Value
children.nullBehavior: the behavior is dependent on usage context

children.label: Headers

children.description:
Configure headers. Entries have lower priority than entries from .headers.
The value is a list of comma separated key-value pairs matching the format of OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/protocol/exporter.md#configuration-options for details.
children.label: Headers List

children.description:
Configure compression.
Known values include: gzip, none. Implementations may support other compression algorithms.
children.label: Compression

children.description:
Configure max time (in milliseconds) to wait for each export.
Value must be non-negative. A value of 0 indicates no limit (infinity).
children.label: Timeout

children.description:
Configure the encoding used for messages. 
Implementations may not support json.
children.label: Encoding

children.description:
Configure max attribute value size. Overrides .attribute_limits.attribute_value_length_limit. 
Value must be non-negative.
children.label: Attribute Value Length Limit

children.description:
Configure max attribute count. Overrides .attribute_limits.attribute_count_limit.

children.label: Attribute Count Limit

children.defaultBehavior: default values as described in LogRecordLimits are used
children.description: Configure log record limits. See also attribute_limits.
children.label: Limits

children.description: Configure if the logger is enabled or not.
children.label: Enabled

children.description:
Configure severity filtering.
Log records with an non-zero (i.e. unspecified) severity number which is less than minimum_severity are not processed.
children.label: Minimum Severity

children.defaultBehavior: trace based filtering is not applied
children.description:
Configure trace based filtering.
If true, log records associated with unsampled trace contexts traces are not processed. If false, or if a log record is not associated with a trace context, trace based filtering is not applied.
children.label: Trace Based

children.defaultBehavior: unmatched .loggers use default values as described in ExperimentalLoggerConfig
children.description: Configure the default logger config used there is no matching entry in .logger_configurator/development.loggers.
children.label: Default Config

children.description: Configure loggers.

Configure logger names to match, evaluated as follows:

 * If the logger name exactly matches.
 * If the logger name matches the wildcard pattern, where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any number of characters including none.
children.label: Name

children.description: Configure temporality preference.

enumOptions.description: Use cumulative aggregation temporality for all instrument types.

children.description: Configure Prometheus Exporter to produce metrics without a target info metric for the resource.
children.label: Without Target Info

children.description:
Configure list of value patterns to include.
Values are evaluated to match as follows:
 * If the value exactly matches.
 * If the value matches the wildcard pattern, where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any number of characters including none.
children.label: Included

children.description:
Configure list of value patterns to exclude. Applies after .included (i.e. excluded has higher priority than included).
Values are evaluated to match as follows:
 * If the value exactly matches.
 * If the value matches the wildcard pattern, where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any number of characters including none.
children.label: Excluded

children.description: Configure Prometheus Exporter to add resource attributes as metrics attributes, where the resource attribute keys match the patterns.
children.label: With Resource Constant Labels

children.description: Configure how metric names are translated to Prometheus metric names.

enumOptions.description: Special character escaping is enabled. Type and unit suffixes are enabled.
Special character escaping is enabled. Type and unit suffixes are disabled. This represents classic Prometheus metric name compatibility.
Special character escaping is disabled. Type and unit suffixes are enabled.
Special character escaping is disabled. Type and unit suffixes are disabled. Metric names are unaltered.

children.label: Translation Strategy

children.label: Instrument Type

children.description: Configure the instrument unit selection criteria.
children.label: Unit

children.description: Configure meter version selection criteria.
children.label: Meter Version

children.label: Meter Schema Url

children.description:
Configure view selector. 
Selection criteria is additive as described in https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#instrument-selection-criteria.
children.label: Selector

children.defaultBehavior: the instrument's original name is used
children.description: Configure metric name of the resulting stream(s).
children.label: Name

children.defaultBehavior: the instrument's origin description is used
children.description: Configure metric description of the resulting stream(s).
children.label: Description

children.description: Configure aggregation of the resulting stream(s).
children.label: Aggregation

options.description: Configures the stream to use the instrument kind to select an aggregation and advisory parameters to influence aggregation configuration parameters. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#default-aggregation for details.
options.label: Default

options.description: Configures the stream to ignore/drop all instrument measurements. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#drop-aggregation for details.
options.label: Drop

children.description: Configure bucket boundaries.
children.label: Boundaries

children.description: Configure record min and max.
children.label: Record Min Max

options.description: Configures the stream to collect data for the histogram metric point using a set of explicit boundary values for histogram bucketing. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#explicit-bucket-histogram-aggregation for details
options.label: Explicit Bucket Histogram

children.description: Configure the max scale factor.
children.label: Max Scale

children.description: Configure the maximum number of buckets in each of the positive and negative ranges, not counting the special zero bucket.
children.label: Max Size

children.description: Configure whether or not to record min and max.
children.label: Record Min Max

options.description: Configures the stream to collect data for the exponential histogram metric point, which uses a base-2 exponential formula to determine bucket boundaries and an integer scale parameter to control resolution. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#base2-exponential-bucket-histogram-aggregation for details.
options.label: Base2 Exponential Bucket Histogram

options.description: Configures the stream to collect data using the last measurement. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#last-value-aggregation for details.
options.label: Last Value

options.description: Configures the stream to collect the arithmetic sum of measurement values. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md#sum-aggregation for details.
options.label: Sum

children.defaultBehavior: the metric reader's default cardinality limit is used
children.description: Configure the aggregation cardinality limit.
children.label: Aggregation Cardinality Limit

children.description:
Configure list of value patterns to include.
Values are evaluated to match as follows:
 * If the value exactly matches.
 * If the value matches the wildcard pattern, where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any number of characters including none.
children.label: Included

children.description:
Configure list of value patterns to exclude. Applies after .included (i.e. excluded has higher priority than included).

 * If the value exactly matches.
 * If the value matches the wildcard pattern, where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any number of characters including none.
children.label: Excluded

enumOptions.description: ExemplarFilter which makes all measurements eligible for being an Exemplar.
ExemplarFilter which makes no measurements eligible for being an Exemplar.
ExemplarFilter which makes measurements recorded in the context of a sampled parent span eligible for being an Exemplar.

children.label: Exemplar Filter

children.description: Configure meters.

Configure meter names to match, evaluated as follows:

 * If the meter name exactly matches.
 * If the meter name matches the wildcard pattern, where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any number of characters including none.

children.description: Configure if the meter is enabled or not.

children.defaultBehavior: all meters use default values as described in ExperimentalMeterConfig

children.defaultBehavior: a noop meter provider is used

children.defaultBehavior: and .composite_list is omitted or null, a noop propagator is used
children.description:
Configure the propagators in the composite text map propagator. Entries from .composite_list are appended to the list here with duplicates filtered out.
Built-in propagator keys include: tracecontext, baggage, b3, b3multi. Known third party keys include: xray.

options.description: Include the w3c trace context propagator.

options.description: Include the w3c baggage propagator.

options.description: Include the zipkin b3 propagator.

options.description: Include the zipkin b3 multi propagator.

children.label: Composite

children.defaultBehavior: and .composite is omitted or null, a noop propagator is used
children.description:
Configure the propagators in the composite text map propagator. Entries are appended to .composite with duplicates filtered out.
The value is a comma separated list of propagator identifiers matching the format of OTEL_PROPAGATORS. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/configuration/sdk-environment-variables.md#general-sdk-configuration for details.
Built-in propagator identifiers include: tracecontext, baggage, b3, b3multi. Known third party identifiers include: xray.
children.label: Composite List

children.description: Configure text map context propagators.
children.label: Propagator

children.description: Configure span processors.

Configure delay interval (in milliseconds) between two consecutive exports. 
Value must be non-negative.
children.label: Schedule Delay

children.description:
Configure maximum allowed time (in milliseconds) to export data. 
Value must be non-negative. A value of 0 indicates no limit (infinity).
children.label: Export Timeout

children.description: Configure maximum queue size. Value must be positive.
children.label: Max Queue Size

children.description: Configure maximum batch size. Value must be positive.
children.label: Max Export Batch Size

children.defaultBehavior: system default certificate verification is used for secure connections

children.description:

children.defaultBehavior: system default TLS settings are used

options.description: Configure sampler to be always_off.
options.label: Always Off

options.description: Configure sampler to be always_on.
options.label: Always On

options.description: Configure sampler to be composite.

options.description: Configure sampler to be always_off.

children.description: Sampler to use when there is no parent.

options.description: Configure sampler to be parent_threshold.
options.label: Parent Threshold

children.description: Configure ratio.
children.label: Ratio

options.description: Configure sampler to be probability.
options.label: Probability

children.description:
The rules for the sampler, matched in order.
Each rule can have multiple match conditions. All conditions must match for the rule to match.
If no conditions are specified, the rule matches all spans that reach it.
If no rules match, the span is not sampled.
The attribute key to match against.
children.label: Key

children.description: The attribute values to match against. If the attribute's value matches any of these, it matches.
children.label: Values

children.description:
Values to match against a single attribute. Non-string attributes are matched using their string representation:
for example, a value of "404" would match the http.response.status_code 404. For array attributes, if any
item matches, it is considered a match.
children.label: Attribute Values

children.description: The attribute key to match against.
children.label: Key

children.description:
Configure list of value patterns to include.
Values are evaluated to match as follows:
 * If the value exactly matches.
 * If the value matches the wildcard pattern, where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any number of characters including none.
children.label: Included

children.description:
Configure list of value patterns to exclude. Applies after .included (i.e. excluded has higher priority than included).
Values are evaluated to match as follows:
 * If the value exactly matches.
 * If the value matches the wildcard pattern, where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any number of characters including none.
children.label: Excluded

children.description:
Patterns to match against a single attribute. Non-string attributes are matched using their string representation:
for example, a pattern of "4*" would match any http.response.status_code in 400-499. For array attributes, if any
item matches, it is considered a match.
children.label: Attribute Patterns

children.description: The span kinds to match. If the span's kind matches any of these, it matches.
children.label: Span Kinds

children.description: The parent span types to match.
children.label: Parent

children.description: The sampler to use for matching spans.
children.label: Sampler

itemSchema.description:
A rule for ExperimentalComposableRuleBasedSampler. A rule can have multiple match conditions - the sampler will be applied if all match. 
If no conditions are specified, the rule matches all spans that reach it.
itemSchema.label: Item

children.label: Rules

children.defaultBehavior: the latest stable version is used, or if no stable version is available and .experimental is true then the latest experimental version is used
children.description: The target semantic convention version for this domain (e.g., 1).
children.label: Version

children.description: Use latest experimental semantic conventions (before stable is available or to enable experimental features on top of stable conventions).
children.label: Experimental

children.description:
When true, also emit the previous major version alongside the target version.
For version=1, the previous version refers to the pre-stable conventions that the instrumentation emitted before the first stable semantic convention version was defined.
For version=2 and above, the previous version is the prior stable major version (e.g., version=2, dual_emit=true emits both v2 and v1).
Enables dual-emit for phased migration between versions.
children.label: Dual Emit

children.defaultBehavior: uses the general stability_opt_in_list setting, or instrumentations continue emitting their default semantic convention version if not set
children.description:

This property takes precedence over the .instrumentation/development.general.stability_opt_in_list setting.

See messaging semantic conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/messaging/
children.label: Messaging

children.defaultBehavior: the latest stable version is used, or if no stable version is available and .experimental is true then the latest experimental version is used
children.description: The target semantic convention version for this domain (e.g., 1).
children.label: Version

children.description: Use latest experimental semantic conventions (before stable is available or to enable experimental features on top of stable conventions).
children.label: Experimental

children.description:
When true, also emit the previous major version alongside the target version.
For version=1, the previous version refers to the pre-stable conventions that the instrumentation emitted before the first stable semantic convention version was defined.
For version=2 and above, the previous version is the prior stable major version (e.g., version=2, dual_emit=true emits both v2 and v1).
Enables dual-emit for phased migration between versions.
children.label: Dual Emit

children.defaultBehavior: uses the general stability_opt_in_list setting, or instrumentations continue emitting their default semantic convention version if not set
children.description:
Configure RPC semantic convention version and migration behavior.

This property takes precedence over the .instrumentation/development.general.stability_opt_in_list setting.

See RPC semantic conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/rpc/
children.label: Semconv

children.defaultBehavior: defaults as described in ExperimentalRpcInstrumentation are used
children.description:
Configure instrumentations following the RPC semantic conventions.
See RPC semantic conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/rpc/
children.label: Rpc

children.defaultBehavior: the default sensitive query parameter list as defined by the url semantic conventions (https://github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conventions/blob/main/docs/registry/attributes/url.md) is used
children.description:
List of query parameter names whose values should be redacted from URLs.
Query parameter names are case-sensitive.
This is a full override of the default sensitive query parameter keys, it is not a list of keys in addition to the defaults.
Set to an empty array to disable query parameter redaction.
children.label: Sensitive Query Parameters

children.defaultBehavior: defaults as described in ExperimentalUrlSanitization are used
children.description: Configure URL sanitization options.
children.label: Url

children.defaultBehavior: defaults as described in ExperimentalSanitization are used
children.description: Configure general sanitization options.
children.label: Sanitization

children.defaultBehavior: no opt-in is configured and instrumentations continue emitting their default semantic convention version
children.description:
Configure semantic convention stability opt-in as a comma-separated list.
This property follows the format and semantics of the OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN environment variable.
Controls the emission of stable vs. experimental semantic conventions for instrumentation.
This setting is only intended for migrating from experimental to stable semantic conventions.

Known values include:
- http: Emit stable HTTP and networking conventions only
- http/dup: Emit both old and stable HTTP and networking conventions (for phased migration)
- database: Emit stable database conventions only
- database/dup: Emit both old and stable database conventions (for phased migration)
- rpc: Emit stable RPC conventions only
- rpc/dup: Emit both experimental and stable RPC conventions (for phased migration)
- messaging: Emit stable messaging conventions only
- messaging/dup: Emit both old and stable messaging conventions (for phased migration)
- code: Emit stable code conventions only
- code/dup: Emit both old and stable code conventions (for phased migration)

Multiple values can be specified as a comma-separated list (e.g., "http,database/dup").
Additional signal types may be supported in future versions.

Domain-specific semconv properties (e.g., .instrumentation/development.general.db.semconv) take precedence over this general setting.

See:
- HTTP migration: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/non-normative/http-migration/
- Database migration: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/database/
- RPC: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/rpc/
- Messaging: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/messaging/messaging-spans/
children.label: Stability Opt In List

children.defaultBehavior: default values as described in ExperimentalGeneralInstrumentation are used
children.description:
Configure general SemConv options that may apply to multiple languages and instrumentations.
Instrumenation may merge general config options with the language specific configuration at .instrumentation.<language>.
children.label: General

children.description: Configure C++ language-specific instrumentation libraries.

Each entry's key identifies a particular instrumentation library. The corresponding value configures it.

children.description: Configure instrumentation.
children.label: Instrumentation

children.description:
Defines configuration parameters specific to a particular OpenTelemetry distribution or vendor.
This section provides a standardized location for distribution-specific settings
that are not part of the OpenTelemetry configuration model.
It allows vendors to expose their own extensions and general configuration options.
children.label: Distribution
